teamObjective.explanation: My hand is weak and contains only low non-trump clubs, so the best team goal is to lead a low single and give my partner a chance to take control of the trick.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2, 3 cards) may hold a higher non-trump card or a trump to cover a low lead, but this is uncertain since the hand has just started and no cards have been revealed.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents likely hold higher cards or trumps. Player 1 has only 2 cards remaining, suggesting they may be near finishing and could try to win the trick, though their exact cards are unknown.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 is the lowest card, making it the least costly lead if an opponent wins, and it maximizes the chance that the partner can beat the trick while opponents expend higher cards.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Slightly higher than the 3; if the partner has only a 3 or 4, they could not cover it, and it reveals no additional advantage.
Higher off-suit card; more likely to be beaten anyway, and it keeps lower cards that are harder to lead effectively later.
My strongest low card; retaining it is better because a future low lead or follow may benefit from having the highest of these weak cards.

risks: An opponent, especially player 1 with only 2 cards, may win the trick and continue leading.
Partner may not be able to cover, letting an opponent consolidate control.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the lowest card, I minimize the value surrendered if the trick is lost and keep slightly stronger low cards for later opportunities.
